reviews.review_title: Great on PC, Excellent on VR
reviews.review_description: I love the old-school boomer shooters, and I had a great time with this on PC, but they did an amazing job on VR.  Just lots of good old-fashioned entertainment.  Fantastic!

If you just want some mindless demon killing with weird weapons in strange castles with secret rooms, this is your game. It's a bit fast paced so may cause nausea more than slow-walking games. 

I was basically sold after smashing the first enemy. It is satisfying.

Update: just went down a spiral staircase and had to lie down for 10 minutes to avoid puking. I have good VR legs too (flight sims and more!)
Doom,Duke nukem,blood,shadow warrior...hexen,the vibe is real. I'm here to throw axes and chew bubblegum...and I'm all outta gum.

This game is a VR port of the excellent retro styled flatscreen game, and it doesn't pull any punches! It's fast, the levels can get pretty big, a run through took me around 7 hours and there's some falls that made my stomach drop. I love it!
Some crazy trippy and varied environments (you haven't seen it all until you've seen it all the way through) and it's just fun to play. It's a game first, opposed to a vr experience, and that's actually exactly what makes it a unique and amazing vr experience.
Great old school boomer shooter with a great selection of weapons and fast and clean gameplay.

reviews.review_title: PLS PORT MORE GAMES FOR QUEST
reviews.review_description: Love it, great boomer shooter, even better in vr
This is a fun fast pace dungeon crawler. I’m really enjoying it.

reviews.review_title: Great game, little short but awesome
reviews.review_description: Love the game, the art style, world, and combat is cool. I just wish it was a bit longer. Very fun and definitely underrated. Give it a try.

What i like in this game is it feels like the old school doom likes but in vr.
No fancy physics that is more annoying than usefull.
The game is fast, not snail paced like most of the vr games.
It looks like retro graphics but it works very very well in vr.
It is also very polished, no bugs, great performances etc.
Seriously buy this game.

This is everything! 
I hyped it up in my mind in such anticipation I’m thinking I’ve ruined this game for myself already. 

I don’t play sandbox shooters anymore because I did that for a year and I’m shot and uninspired yet they keep coming out one after the other. 

Then Shock Troops came out and that really got me going for ID games and 90’s shooters like Doom and Quake and Duke Nukem.

This game seems to have the whole thing just be DOOM, with the pixelation and the way they do the sky. The hidden rooms and blitz killings. 

The graphics are perfect. I can’t ask for more knowing what the Quest is capable of. It’s perfect for this game. The low res brings you back home. 

But the real deal comes within the game’s design. I mean I thought it was going to get tacky once I saw the checkered level (which I loved) but it only got more out there. I mean it’s just out there for miles. I dare you to name me a shooter more imaginative and outrageous than this one. I love the enemies as the variety is deep. I stopped counting how many different enemies there are, like over 30 maybe? Each section provides a whole slew of them and a brand new motif to each level.

This game is wild! I think there are like 6 different weapons including a pitch fork, mace, sword, staff, planet launcher (yup), and pink squiggly wavy lines subsisting of a weapon. All weapons are projectiles with the exception of the defaulted axe of which I never use.

But seriously, the level design is outrageous. Up is down, down is up. Crazy color schemes, a body of water up in the air in the middle of the room. Water bubbles to travel on, and a flying mechanic. Swirling paths to lead to swirling rooms and into madness. 

It’s not easy describing the indescribable so I will not try to go further into the depth of the levels.

The game is outrageously long, at least for me because I take my time looking for hidden rooms (it’s hard). 

Currently I still haven’t finished the game and it’s beyond 10 hours easy. But the last level I played was so indescribable, I had to write something. 

The music is deep, as in it moves me. It’s so focused on melody and ambiance and does it so well. I wish I could download it somewhere. 

No joke. Absolutely most imaginative shooter I’ve ever played for miles, and one of the more imaginative games in any genre I’ve played. 

You should be advised that I only recommend the hard difficulty as anything else isn’t really hard enough. That is definitely noticeable. Warrior mode will add a challenge to each difficulty starting you off with your weak defaulted axe on each turn leaving all your previously collected weapons behind.

This game is amazing why I didn't purchase earlier? ..so smooth game play is great and controls are spot on especially changing weapons great to do that in slow motion very intuitive thank you for a great game

So when i bought this game i was like "oh it's like ultrakill but vr" so i bought it and god i did not regret it everything is good in it and now i just realised that the ones who made it was new blood interactive and i got so hyped anyways i feel like adding a sandbox map or more mobility like sliding could be really good 
Also THERES MAURICE HECK YEAH 🔥🔥🔥

developer_response.body: WE GOT BIGGER MAURICES THAT MAURICE!

reviews.review_description: It's always been a great game but got even better after the last update.  Beautiful graphics, atmospheric music, mind blowing level design ( the level to the last boss is a real trip) and you also get horde mode which is preety cool too. The only question is when/if we are getting The Black Labyrinth dlc? I played it on PC too and it's exact same game, no cuts to VR so great job. Again , give us The Black Labyrinth ( buying day one).

Never been so compelled to write a review so quickly. Not even played an hour.

Why the 5 stars? If I'm not playing rhythm games I'm shooting stuff, played tonnes of vr fps games and this just had me moving around the map with such precision and control immediately. Absolutely nailed the immediacy and razor sharp twitch movement needed for boomer shooters.

Compound was until now king in this department, Amid EVIL is top tier, and feels more reliable, consistent and tight. Perhaps the best yet?

Can't comment too much on pretty much the rest of the game 😂 but from what I saw the level design is tight and truly in keeping with 90s era map design and the weaponry is far less constricted to melee than the screenshots portray.

Despite my preference for online, truly looking forward to sinking time into this

With the latest update the gameplay is much smoother, controls feel great (specifically using the mace) and the axe throwing is so satisfying especially in the endless mode!

I wish the endless mode saved your score (the highest wave reached and the number of kills)

“Amid Evil VR” is a really brilliantly made retro shooter in the style of the 90s classics “Heretic” & “Hexen”. The flat version is a lot of fun, but in VR this game is a masterpiece. With a cool arsenal of unusual magical weapons, we fend off bizarre hordes of enemies. The level design is fantastically conceived and sometimes quite demanding. "Arcane Expanse" in particular is one of my favorite levels because of its confusing paths. The graphics were designed in pixel optics as befits a retro shooter. The soundtrack is in a class of its own and gives the game a really cool atmosphere. Anyone who loved the boomer shooters of the 90s shouldn't miss this great game.
A solid modern take on the retro style of FPS, and this VR port is definitely well done.  Highly recommend 👍

I like oldschool fps games but apparently this vr version of Amid Evil wasn't tested at all, there are bugs like enemies clipping through walls and making impossible to get 100% kills. Episode 1 map 1 has secret that is impossible to get unless you use teleport to get there. Episode 2 map 3 has secret that's supposed to be in water and pc version it totally works but in this garbage the entrance to secret has some gray floor and you can't swim through it, thus making it impossible to obtain all secrets in that level. There's probably more bugged secrets but this was last straw for me. You really couldn't bother to playtest this vr port before releasing it, makes it feel this was a quick cash grab. All this soured my experience and nobody should support lazy vr port, can't recommend this to anyone.
Classic great game
very snappy and surprisingly without motion sickness, okay graphics but quite immersive, controls are not optimized for VR but good enough, interesting level design, enemy behavior a bit simplistic

Pretty samey, very buggy, wouldn’t fly on a proper platform.

Progression is confusing, possibly broken. At some point I’m brought back to a previously completed level, and I’m honestly not invested enough in this mess to figure out why. Another lazy ‘retro experience’ that isn’t good enough to call a full game. Dummies keep five-starring mediocre stuff like this and keeping the medium disappointing. Gotta get too close to dumb rune hints to get them to translate, so close that you can’t read the translations. That level of attention to detail explains all the other bugs you’ll endure trying to complete this lazy excuse for a game.

reviews.review_title: Regret not going for the Steam version
reviews.review_description:
By far my favorite part of playing through Amid Evil on PC was the levels. They're expansive, imaginative, and beautiful.
With the retro aesthetic I didn't think they would have to downgrade the visuals this much for the Quest 2. Water refracting onto walls, gone. Enemy animations getting culled when far away (remember, huge open spaces are common).

And I wish the Whisper's Edge let me direct the orientation of the slice. So you could do vertical slices etc. That was by far the biggest disappointment on the VR implementation, but in general I don't feel the VR inputs are utilized to any extent. Enemies aren't thrown in the direction of your swing as they ragdoll into pieces.

The best part of this is it's functional, and there's a lot of content to go through.

developer_response.body: We're getting a performance bump on Quest 2 SOON™

reviews.review_title: This game is a total rip-off.
reviews.review_description: This game should be free. No, actually, they should have had to pay people to play this. It’s apparently a port of another game, but that doesn’t excuse how bad of a game it is. It’s a poor excuse for a roguelite, is ridiculously buggy, and has no diversity within enemies or weapons or levels. They couldn’t even be bothered to make proper hand animations and still had the gall to charge $20 dollars for this hot trash. I want my money back.

developer_response.body:
The game isn't a roguelite, and isn't buggy at all, has a ton of unique enemies and weapons - are you sure you are reviewing the right game?!

Edit: bait used to be believavle.

reviews.review_description: Initially I gave it a 4.7/5 after one hour in but the further I get in the game the worse it runs. Not sure if its just me or my quest 2 but in big areas with lots of enemies I get lots of fps drops and lag, ruining the experience. Would appreciate a response from the devs if this issue could be resolved and will change my review accordingly. Its a shame because the game is awesome. All my other games run well
A wonderful game for people like me growing up with Doom, Hexen and Heretic. Phantastic Retro Feeling! Just one little bug I have noticed: Hands doesn't fit 100 % to the real hands on Quest Pro.

developer_response.body: Glad you enjoyed! Spread the word!

reviews.review_description: This is a real gem in the app store. So much depth and imagination. If you like shock troops you will LOVE this

This is like a baby’s game. 
Too much heath just laying around all over the place and it takes away any challenge.

One of easiest most boring FPS games I’ve ever played.

reviews.review_title: Why I Wouldn't Recommend This Game
reviews.review_description: Well, the game isn't complete garbage or anything, but in my opinion, it doesn't deserve 4 or 5 stars. It's way too easy and way too short. And it's just not worth the price for me. I'd recommend "Compound" instead, at least that's a challenge.

developer_response.body:
Too short? Bruh it's like 8 hours long that's a lot - especially for a VR game! And we have more content coming SOON for FREE!

Also if you think it's too easy be sure to find EVIL difficulty ;)

reviews.review_title: Old school QUAKE style on VR LETS GOOOO
reviews.review_description:
Fun boomer shooting on the quest! Trippy cool @$$ graphics on gnarly weapons. Crystallized enemies and labyrinth levels galore. 

***Give us DUSK and Iron Lung pretty please with sugar on top

***had to download 3 separate files to play the game which was weird and annoying, but ultimately worth it.

This game captures the feeling of the Doom/Quake era of FPS shooters. As long as you go in with those expectations you'll have a great time. This is just a port of the flat screen PC game, so there's none of the VR interactivity with the environment. The only complaint I have is the sound effects. I wish the weapons had more audio oomph. Overall this is a fantastic game.

developer_response.body: We will look into adding more OOMPH

reviews.review_description: Please buy

Yes, yes, yes. I love games from New blood. This is a real old-school FPS with a great gameplay. Thank you guys for this masterpiece, please port your other games, I will buy them all without question.
10/10 would destroy the earth again

reviews.review_title: Wanna save some bucks? Send me a message
reviews.review_description: Amazing game especially if you are older and have this nostalgic feeling for the graphic!

I absolutely love playing this retro game with magic weapons! The graphics, the sounds, and the gameplay are all so nostalgic and bring me back to a simpler time. However, as a relatively seasoned VR player, I have to admit that I got extremely dizzy after about 30 minutes of playing. I know that this feeling will go away with time as I get more accustomed to the game, but I just wanted to give a fair warning to others who may experience the same thing. Despite the dizziness, I still highly recommend this game to any fan of retro games and magic weapons!

Repetitive gameplay, gloomy monotonous levels and enemies, weapons that are almost interchangeable with similar ones from the same category, the general style lacks some spice. There's nothing that could catch me. Just my opinion. I was hoping it would be something like Compound, but with magic melee combat, but... no. I feel that the developers have invested their soul in this game, so 3 stars.

If you’re looking for an updated version of a Boomer Shooter style game in VR, this is the game you are looking for.   Loved it.   Brings back memories of playing Doom, Heretic, Hexen and a few others.  This game is super trippy.   With that said it did take me a month of playing in short doses to get beyond a sense of vertigo. But end the end I thoroughly enjoyed this game and am hoping to see Black Labyrinth in the future.

reviews.review_title: New Blood = Retro Shooter + New Tech
reviews.review_description:
As of late I’ve been DEVOURING retro shooters with a relentless appetite. I’d recently tried the 2D computer demo for Amid Evil on Steam and was incredibly happy to see they were making a VR port. I knew it would be good because New Blood has proven so trustworthy thus far I’d trust them with my life. I felt bad for having neglected my Quest in favor of 30 year old games for so these past couple of months, and I felt ecstatic seeing that there would be an opportunity to have my my cake and eat it too! Little did I know, the port was releasing on my birthday! I picked it up right away! 

I say this port nails the formula in VR! Obviously New Blood knows how a retro shooter should work, but it’s impressive how well they got it to work in VR. 
Fast and frenetic without being overwhelming (at least first two levels on Hard). I did turn on Automatic Soul Mode and it does work a bit different than it does on the computer, where Soul Mode is stored until the next attack, but I almost like the way it just uses it immediately in this port.
The weapons are all very cool, and I can’t wait to see The Aeternum (BFG) in VR! The normal sprites on the computer already look hauntingly beautiful! They’ve added a few little VR only functions, like being able to pluck the planets from the Celestial Claw (rocket launcher) and being able to throw them by hand. Throwing your weapons around is kinda fun too. I would say that they ought to give you something to do with your other hand like letting you dual-wield or something, but I know Amid Evil, I know New Blood, and throwing in extra crap to clog up the flow of the game is not conducive to the type of game that Amid Evil VR should be and it would be stupid to ask for it. It’s like telling Michelangelo that the Sistine Chapel￼ would be a lot cooler with a flaming monster truck. A monster truck would be cool, but it would be so out of place that it would cease to be cool and would detract from the overall vision and quality of the masterpiece.

LITTLE Nitpicks: Sometimes text is difficult to read. Usually you are way too close to be able to read the full message for in game text, and the in menu text is a little too blurry. That menu text looks like someone stepped on it. It could just be the Quest questing down the resolution, but I feel like maybe the menu text should have been larger in that case. 
Even when playing the computer version, I found the screen flashing for pickups and weapon fire a bit annoying, in VR it can be a little obnoxious but you can also get so immersed you don’t mind. 
One very valid nitpick I’ve seen from others is that the sensitivity on the grip buttons is too high- and yeah, I had some accidental juggling moments too. I typically think that inputs should be able to be adjusted on a slider, I wouldn’t be surprised if we see something like that in the future (if they don’t just reduce the sensitivity to some arbitrary value, doesn’t really matter though). So they might end up patching some things later. All in all, not bad for day zero of New Blood Interactive’s first(?) VR game! Bravo to Indefatigable for putting it all together in such a wonderful and stable initial release!

I have seen some rather silly reviews on here, like people somehow being confused about having to pistol-start after dying, just reload your save! Or try not dying! I only died for the first time on E1M3 because I was ready to quit and wanted to see how many enemies I could kill just from throwing my weapons (it pretty much one hits the imps even on hard, but the big guys are a bit of a seventh inning stretch). 
These reviewers didn’t exactly seem like they knew how this type of game is supposed to work. 

I think the only Quest Store game that gets even close to the retro shooter category is Compound, and that’s only really when you use the “Old School” mutator. You won’t hear me complain about Compound because I like it too. If you are a retro shooter fan who hasn’t tried it yet, seek it out. You CAN get the same feeling from it. It’s pretty good.

I still need to grab the New Blood Interactive triple pack on Steam. Indefatigable and New Blood will both be getting more money from me shortly!

I think a higher difficulty mode might be nice (or I’m going to get my face curb stomped for my hubris).

I never do reviews, but I wanted to bring up the rating average of a New Blood title. Imaging not having a 5/5! Frightening!
Old fashion look. Awesome weapons to unlock. I am having a blast. Secret rooms to find like the old games like golden axe but in 3D. Worth every single penny

reviews.review_title: This game is gonna be amazing
reviews.review_description:
I'm a big fan of all of these retro style games and I'm so excited to play it.
